Platform & Data.narrative: This family ensures your ERP data is always safe, accessible from any device, and compliant with Singapore's data protection laws. SMEs depend on this to keep operations running even during outages, serve customers in multiple languages, and protect sensitive business information from breaches. A retail chain needs mobile access for floor staff to check inventory; a services firm must back up client data daily to meet PDPA requirements.

Productivity & Collaboration.narrative: These capabilities connect your team's conversations, schedules, and knowledge in one place so information doesn't get lost in email threads or scattered documents. SMEs benefit by reducing time spent searching for answers and keeping customer interactions visible across the business. A sales team using integrated email and calendar can track all client touchpoints; a support team using internal messaging resolves issues faster without email delays.

Workflow & Automation.narrative: This family automates repetitive approval steps, sends timely alerts, and centralizes documents so processes run faster and with fewer errors. SMEs save time on manual handoffs and reduce bottlenecks—especially critical when staff are stretched thin. A purchase order that auto-routes to the right approver based on amount, or a leave request that notifies the manager instantly, keeps the business moving without constant follow-ups.

Intelligence & Analytics.narrative: These tools turn raw ERP data into visual dashboards, AI-powered insights, and compliance records so you can make faster decisions and prove you're following regulations. SMEs use this to spot trends (declining sales, inventory issues) before they become crises and to demonstrate good governance to auditors or regulators. A distributor tracking sales by region in real time; a manufacturer using AI to flag unusual purchase patterns.

Customisation & Integration.narrative: This family lets you tailor the ERP to your unique business without hiring developers, connect it to your existing tools, and manage data quality across the system. SMEs need this flexibility to grow without being locked into rigid processes or expensive custom coding. A multi-branch retailer running separate P&Ls from one system; a firm integrating its ERP with a third-party logistics platform via API.
